AnswerRequest.description: The body of ``POST /questions/{id}/answer`` — the human's answer.
AnswerRequest.title: AnswerRequest

AnswerResult.description:
The answer write's outcome — first-write-wins CAS.

``won`` is True for the write that landed the row; the loser gets ``won=False`` with
the **winning** row so it can be told who already answered (the 409 body).
AnswerResult.title: AnswerResult

ApplyResponse.description:
The response to a completion submission.

Exactly one of ``next_envelope`` (when ``outcome == next``) or ``detail`` (on a
non-advancing outcome) is meaningful; the ``outcome`` discriminates.
ApplyResponse.title: ApplyResponse

ArtifactView.description:
One entry of a chunk's inline artifact store.

``key`` is the store key ``{node}.{artifact-name}.{epoch}`` — append-only, so
every re-run's entry is retained and latest-by-epoch resolution is the reader's.
``content`` carries an **asset's** text verbatim (a review's findings
document); the ``repo``/``branch_name``/``commit_hash`` trio carries a
``git_commit`` artifact's pinned reference (the hub stores the reference, never the
code).

``branch_url`` is the forge ``tree`` URL for the produced branch, resolved server-side
from the chunk's issue-shaped PM pointer so the board can link a ``git_commit``
to the branch on the forge; null when no forge web base is derivable — the row then
shows the branch name without a link (no broken link).

``recorded_at`` is the instant the artifact was attached, decoded from its id's
ULID timestamp (the store keeps no separate column); null for a malformed id.
ArtifactView.title: ArtifactView

CheckResult.description: One deterministic check's outcome, informing the verdict.
CheckResult.title: CheckResult

ChunkDetail.description:
The chunk aggregate in full — the board's chunk view and envelope feed.

Carries the chunk's **transition history** and its inline **artifact store** so the
web app can render every node it visited, the review that failed once and looped
back to build, and the artifacts — the branch pointers merged and the review notes.
ChunkDetail.title: ChunkDetail

ChunkGraphUpdateRequest.description: Repin a not-ready chunk's workflow graph (issue #27) — the target graph's id.
ChunkGraphUpdateRequest.title: ChunkGraphUpdateRequest

ChunkGraphView.description: A chunk's current graph selection — the read/write shape issue #27's board editor uses.
ChunkGraphView.title: ChunkGraphView

ChunkGroupRequest.description:
Merge unacquired chunks into one — the board's Group control.

``merge_chunk_ids`` are the ready chunks folded into the path's survivor chunk; the
survivor absorbs the union of their PM pointers and the merged chunks are discarded as
ephemeral. Self-references and duplicates are ignored; a non-ready member is
rejected ``409``.
ChunkGroupRequest.title: ChunkGroupRequest

ChunkGroupResponse.description: The survivor chunk after a group — its id and the union of PM pointers it carries.
ChunkGroupResponse.title: ChunkGroupResponse

ChunkIngestRequest.description:
Ingest by source-native token — specific items always, batch fine.

Each token is resolved against the configured PM sources' own grammar
(``IPmSource.parse``): ``{name}:{ref}``, ``{name}#{ref}``, or the item's own URL.
Tokens only — no pre-resolved ``{source, ref}`` shape travels alongside them; a
second intake shape would reintroduce exactly the config-blind guess that
resolving against the configured sources removes.
ChunkIngestRequest.title: ChunkIngestRequest

ChunkModelUpdateRequest.description: Repin a not-ready chunk's model selection (issue #27).
ChunkModelUpdateRequest.title: ChunkModelUpdateRequest

ChunkModelView.description: A chunk's current model selection — the read/write shape issue #27's board editor uses.
ChunkModelView.title: ChunkModelView

ChunkPauseRequest.description: Set or clear a chunk's operator pause brake — records who flipped it (issue #46).
ChunkPauseRequest.title: ChunkPauseRequest

ChunkStatus.description: The derived chunk statuses. Never stored — always a query result.
ChunkStatus.title: ChunkStatus

ChunkSummary.description:
One row of the fleet chunk list — derived status + current node.

``current_node_name`` is the node's human graph name (``build``, ``review``) the
board renders in place of the raw ``nd_`` ULID; null when the chunk has no
current node or its pinned graph cannot resolve the id.

Deliberately status-only: the summary feeds the board **card**, which is a passive
status view (issue #42), so no operator *fact* is carried here. The pause fact — and
every other fact an operator action keys on — reaches the chunk detail dock through
:class:`ChunkDetail`, the one place a board action lives. ``runner_id`` (the live
route's holder, null when unrouted) is a passive where-is-it fact in that same
sense — it lets the fleet registry list each runner's claims — not an action key.
``cost`` is the one exception (issue #59): the derived spend total is cheap to carry
on every card and is not itself an operator fact, so it rides the summary rather than
waiting for the detail fetch.
ChunkSummary.title: ChunkSummary

ChunkUsageTotalView.description:
A chunk's derived usage/cost total — summed over every recorded invocation
(issue #59). Never a stored column: computed at read time by
``derive_chunk_usage``.

``cost_partial`` carries the lower-bound + PARTIAL contract on ``cost_usd`` —
see :class:`~blizzard.hub.domain.work.UsageTotal` for the one canonical
statement of it, which this view's fields mirror verbatim.
ChunkUsageTotalView.title: ChunkUsageTotalView

ChunkUsageView.description:
One node-step's usage/cost telemetry (issue #59) — one harness invocation's
tokens-by-class and cost, oldest first on :class:`ChunkDetail`.

``cost_usd`` is ``None`` exactly when no result envelope existed for this
invocation (the envelope-less transcript-summation fallback) — never fabricated.
ChunkUsageView.title: ChunkUsageView

CompletionSubmission.description: A node-step's completion — judgement choice + checks + artifacts + epoch.
CompletionSubmission.title: CompletionSubmission

DecisionChoiceModel.description: One selectable gate outcome — a button on the board/bot.
DecisionChoiceModel.title: DecisionChoiceModel

DecisionResolutionRequest.description: A person's choice for an open decision — first-write-wins CAS.
DecisionResolutionRequest.title: DecisionResolutionRequest

DecisionResolutionResponse.description: The winning resolution — the choice, who, and when.
DecisionResolutionResponse.title: DecisionResolutionResponse

DecisionSubmission.description:
A runner-config gate: submit a decision in place of a transition.

Carries the gated step's artifacts and its fencing epoch — one atomic, epoch-fenced
write, exactly where a worker-judged node would have submitted its transition. The
node's choice set is supplied by the hub from the pinned graph, not sent here.
DecisionSubmission.title: DecisionSubmission

DecisionView.description:
A gate decision in full — the board's card and the runner's pickup.

``resolved_choice`` is set once a person has decided; ``transitioned`` is true once
the holding runner has recorded the resolving transition. The runner acts on a
decision that is resolved but not yet transitioned.
DecisionView.title: DecisionView

EnvelopeArtifact.description: One artifact carried into a node-step, resolved latest-by-epoch.
EnvelopeArtifact.title: EnvelopeArtifact

EnvelopeChoice.description: A selectable outcome the worker's judgement may emit.
EnvelopeChoice.title: EnvelopeChoice

EscalationReport.description:
A runner's ``escalation.recorded`` — retries exhausted for the node.

``takeover_command`` is the literal ``cd <workdir> && <harness resume>`` a human
pastes to enter the parked session; ``epoch`` is the
exhausted attempt's fence, closed by a later lease mint.
EscalationReport.title: EscalationReport

EscalationView.description:
An open escalation on a ``needs_human`` chunk.

Surfaces the runner-composed takeover command so a human can resume the parked
session. Present only while the escalation is open —
a later lease mint (requeue/takeover) supersedes it and this drops away.
EscalationView.title: EscalationView

FleetSpendView.description:
The fleet's usage/cost total since ``since``. ``cost_partial`` carries the
lower-bound + PARTIAL contract on ``cost_usd`` — see
:class:`~blizzard.hub.domain.work.UsageTotal` for the one canonical statement of
it, which this view's fields mirror verbatim.
FleetSpendView.title: FleetSpendView

GraphMintRequest.description: A graph definition to mint — the raw YAML body.
GraphMintRequest.title: GraphMintRequest

GraphNodeView.description: A reified node in a minted graph.
GraphNodeView.title: GraphNodeView

GraphView.description: A minted graph as served by ``GET /graphs`` and the mint response.
GraphView.title: GraphView

JudgedBy.description: Who renders a node's exit judgement — the structural gate marker.
JudgedBy.title: JudgedBy

LeaseMintReport.description: A runner's ``lease.minted`` — one node-step attempt's fencing epoch.
LeaseMintReport.title: LeaseMintReport

NodeConfig.description: The node's invariant identity for this step.
NodeConfig.title: NodeConfig

NodeEnvelope.description: Everything a runner needs to work one node-step.
NodeEnvelope.title: NodeEnvelope

OpenDecisionsResponse.description: The fleet's open (unresolved) decisions — ``blizzard hub decisions``.
OpenDecisionsResponse.title: OpenDecisionsResponse

PauseView.description:
An open pause on a chunk (issue #46) — who set it and when.

Present only while ``paused=True`` is the newest pause fact; a resume clears it.
Carried independently of ``status``: PAUSED sits below the human-gated statuses in
the derivation order, so a chunk both paused and parked on a question derives
``waiting_on_human`` — this field is the only way the runner (and the board) learn
the chunk is paused in that case, and it also answers "who paused it".
PauseView.title: PauseView

PmItemEntry.description:
One pointer's pass-through PM item — title, body + comment
thread, vendor-native.

``label``/``web_url`` are the board-legible pointer label (``blizzard#8``) and its
browser address — both null when no configured source names ``source``. A
per-pointer forge failure degrades here rather than failing the whole read:
``error`` carries the reason and ``title``/``body`` are null, so one unreachable
pointer never blinds the reader to the pointers it did reach.
PmItemEntry.title: PmItemEntry

PmItemsView.description:
A chunk's pass-through PM items — one entry per pointer, order preserved.

Empty when the chunk holds no pointers — the board's empty state; a grouped chunk carrying
many pointers yields one entry per pointer, each fetched fresh and never stored.
PmItemsView.title: PmItemsView

PmPointerModel.description:
One ``{source, ref}`` PM pointer — ``source`` names a configured
``[[pm_source]]``; ``ref`` is that source's own item token.
PmPointerModel.title: PmPointerModel

PmPointerView.description:
A pointer as the views render it — the raw pair plus its legible
label and browser URL, both rendered by the pointer's configured source binding.

``label`` is the board-legible ``{name}#{ref}`` (e.g. ``blizzard#8``); ``web_url``
is its browser-openable address. Both null when no configured source names
``source`` — the board then leans on the chunk's stable short id instead.
PmPointerView.title: PmPointerView

PrView.description: An open PR a chunk is parked on in open-pr delivery mode.
PrView.title: PrView

QuestionAsked.description:
A ``question.asked`` fact the runner forwards to the hub.

``question_id`` is runner-minted (``qn_<ulid>``) so the runner can poll the answer
back by it; ``epoch`` is the parked lease's fence, ``session_id`` the dormant
session to resume around the answer, and ``options`` the choices the board renders.
QuestionAsked.title: QuestionAsked

QuestionView.description:
A question row with its derived answer state — the surfacing shape.

Behind ``GET /questions`` (open only), ``GET /questions/{id}`` (the runner's answer
poll), and the chunk detail's open-questions list. ``answered`` and the answer
fields derive from the presence of the answer row.
QuestionView.title: QuestionView

QueuePeekEntry.description: One ready chunk, in queue order.
QueuePeekEntry.title: QueuePeekEntry

QueuePeekResponse.description: The ready queue as peeked by FILL, in the hub's explicit order.
QueuePeekResponse.title: QueuePeekResponse

QueueReorderRequest.description:
Move a ready chunk to a queue position — the board's Prioritize control.

``position`` is the target index in the ready queue, ``0`` being the top; it is
clamped into range, so ``0`` always means "to the front". Ordering is a hub-side
property: the move appends one position fact and the order re-derives.
QueueReorderRequest.title: QueueReorderRequest

QueueReorderResponse.description: The ready queue after a reorder, in its new order — the board re-renders from it.
QueueReorderResponse.title: QueueReorderResponse

ReadinessResponse.description: The wire shape of a readiness reading (openapi-ts consumes this).
ReadinessResponse.title: ReadinessResponse

RouteClaim.description: A complete route fact posted by the claiming runner.
RouteClaim.title: RouteClaim

RouteClaimResponse.description: The winning claim's reply — the route plus the first node envelope.
RouteClaimResponse.title: RouteClaimResponse

RouteView.description: A chunk's route — where it is being worked.
RouteView.title: RouteView

RunnerFact.description:
One buffered runner fact: its per-runner seq, its kind, and its payload.

``payload`` is the kind-specific body — for ``lease.minted`` ``{chunk_id, epoch}``,
for ``escalation.recorded`` ``{chunk_id, epoch, takeover_command}`` — kept open so a
new runner fact kind bolts on without a wire change.
RunnerFact.title: RunnerFact

RunnerFactAck.description:
The hub's per-batch acknowledgement against its high-water mark.

``high_water`` is the runner's new mark after this batch; ``applied`` and
``already_applied`` partition the pushed seqs so the runner can ack its buffer
(a semantic rejection still acks — rejection is an outcome, not a delivery
failure). ``rejected`` names seqs the hub refused for a non-idempotency
reason (an unknown kind), which the runner surfaces rather than silently drops.
RunnerFactAck.title: RunnerFactAck

RunnerFactBatch.description: A runner's push of one-or-more buffered facts, ordered by seq.
RunnerFactBatch.title: RunnerFactBatch

RunnerListResponse.description: The fleet registry — every registered runner with its liveness.
RunnerListResponse.title: RunnerListResponse

RunnerPauseRequest.description: Set a runner's pause brake — records who flipped it.
RunnerPauseRequest.title: RunnerPauseRequest

RunnerRegistrationRequest.description: Register a runner into the fleet — runner id + workspace binding.
RunnerRegistrationRequest.title: RunnerRegistrationRequest

RunnerRegistrationResponse.description: The registered runner's id, and whether this call first created its row.
RunnerRegistrationResponse.title: RunnerRegistrationResponse

RunnerView.description:
One fleet-registry row — derived liveness and both brakes.

A runner can be paused by two different parties for two different reasons, so the two
are reported separately rather than collapsed into one ``paused`` (issue #43): the
board shows *which*. A reader that wants "is it claiming?" ORs them; since issue #45
the two diverge past claiming — ``hub_paused`` keeps its claims-only meaning, while
``locally_paused`` alone answers "is it spawning anything at all?".
RunnerView.title: RunnerView

SubmittedArtifact.description: An artifact committed atomically with the completion.
SubmittedArtifact.title: SubmittedArtifact

TransitionView.description:
One accepted transition in a chunk's history.

The edge a node-step took — its origin node, the judgement choice that routed it,
and its destination — oldest first on the detail. This is what makes the review-fail
loop legible: a ``review -> build`` entry with ``choice_name = "fail"`` is a visible
step in the timeline (MVP criterion 9/11).

``from_node_name``/``to_node_name`` are the nodes' human graph names (``build``,
``review``) the board renders in place of the raw ``nd_`` ULIDs; resolved here so the
timeline is legible without reassembly, null when the pinned graph cannot
resolve the id.
TransitionView.title: TransitionView

get.description: The fleet chunk list — derived status per chunk.

schema.title: Response List Chunks Api Chunks Get

post.description:
Ingest by source-native token; 422 on a token no configured source
claims; 409 on a pointer held by a live chunk.

get.description: One chunk aggregate in full — derived status, current node, route.

post.description:
Poll a parked open-pr chunk's PRs; finalize the delivery once all are terminal.

The on-demand external-merge detection (the impatient path): for a chunk parked in
``open-pr`` mode, check every open PR through the forge and, when all have merged or
closed, write the terminal facts so the chunk flips to ``done`` and its environments
release. A no-op when the chunk has no open PR or is already finalized.
Apply a node-step's completion atomically; reply carries the next envelope.
Runner-config gate: park the chunk on a decision in place of a transition.
Forcibly release a chunk from its runner without touching any escalation.

schema.title: Response Detach Chunk Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Detach Post

get.description: The chunk's current node envelope, idempotent — the lost-apply re-read.

post.description: Land a runner's ``escalation.recorded`` — the chunk derives ``needs_human``.

schema.title: Response Report Escalation Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Escalations Post

post.description:
Repin a not-ready chunk's workflow graph (issue #27).

404 on an unknown chunk or an unknown target graph; 409 once the chunk has left
``not_ready`` (already promoted, claimed, running, or later).
Merge unacquired chunks into ``chunk_id`` — the board's Group control.
Land a runner's ``lease.minted`` — keeps the epoch fence in lockstep.

schema.title: Response Report Lease Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Leases Post

post.description:
Repin a not-ready chunk's model selection (issue #27).

404 on an unknown chunk; 422 on a blank model; 409 once the chunk has left
``not_ready`` (already promoted, claimed, running, or later).
Set a chunk's operator pause brake — the claim is kept, unlike detach (issue #46).

schema.title: Response Pause Chunk Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Pause Post

get.description:
Pass-through PM items read — one entry per pointer, contents never stored.

Each pointer is resolved to its own binding by name (``pm.get(pointer.source)``), then
fetched fresh from the forge; a per-pointer resolution or forge failure degrades to an
``error`` on that entry rather than failing the whole read, so a grouped chunk
still surfaces the pointers it reached beside a notice for the ones it did not. A chunk
with no pointers is an empty list — the board's empty state — not a 404. No configured
PM source at all is a 503 up front — the request-wide degradation preserved unchanged
from before per-pointer resolution existed.

post.description:
Promote a not-ready chunk to ready so a runner may claim it.

Idempotent: promoting an already-ready or already-running chunk is a harmless no-op.
404 only when the chunk is unknown.

schema.title: Response Promote Chunk Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Promote Post

post.description: Close an escalation by supersession: requeue at the current node.

schema.title: Response Requeue Chunk Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Requeues Post

post.description: Clear a chunk's operator pause brake — idempotent, never refused (issue #46).

schema.title: Response Resume Chunk Api Chunks  Chunk Id  Resume Post

get.description: The fleet's open (unresolved) decisions — gate surfacing.

post.description: Resolve an open decision, first-write-wins CAS.

Land runner-minted facts, idempotent by per-runner seq high-water.

The store-and-forward ingest: ``lease.minted`` (the fence input), ``escalation.recorded``,
``question.asked``, and ``answer.delivered`` ride the runner's outbound buffer here. A
pushed seq at or below the runner's high-water mark is already-applied and re-acked; a
fresh one is applied and advances the mark. Each freshly-applied fact re-broadcasts on
the SSE stream so the board refreshes — ``chunk-changed`` for every touched chunk, and
``question-asked`` for a forwarded ask.

get.description:
The fleet's total usage/cost since ``since`` (an ISO-8601 instant) — summed
over every usage fact recorded at or after it, across every chunk.

post.description: Validate and mint an immutable graph; 422 on validation errors.

get.description: Every open (unanswered) question across the fleet — the ``hub status`` surface.

schema.title: Response List Open Questions Api Questions Get

post.description: Land a ``question.asked`` row — the chunk parks ``waiting_on_human``.

schema.title: Response Ask Question Api Questions Post

get.description: One question with its derived answer state — the runner's answer poll.

post.description: Answer a question first-write-wins; 409 carries the winning answer.

get.description: The hub-ordered ready queue, read-only — honours reorder + grouping.

post.description: Move a ready chunk to a queue position — the board's Prioritize control.

Claim a chunk; 403 if the runner is paused at the hub, 409 if already claimed,
else the first node envelope.

get.description: The fleet registry — every runner with derived liveness + paused state.

post.description: Register a runner — runner id + workspace binding; idempotent upsert.

get.description: One runner's declarative state — the runner's own pull read.

post.description: Refresh a runner's liveness — the slow runner-level heartbeat. Returns 204.
Set a runner's pause brake — no new claims; in-flight chunks run on.
Clear a runner's pause brake — it resumes claiming on its next pull.
